nodes.context: International outlaw motorcycle club involved in drug trafficking and violent conflicts with other clubs.
Early motorcycle club from which Otto Friedli broke to form Hells Angels.
Motorcycle club whose member Dick White allegedly founded Hells Angels.
Motorcycle club whose former members founded the San Francisco Hells Angels charter.
Biker club whose former members James and Pat Miles formed a Sacramento Hells Angels charter.
19th-century American outlaw gang, used as a romanticized comparison to Hells Angels.
Black motorcycle club based in Oakland, California, with a friendship with the Hells Angels.
African-American motorcycle club that had friendly relations with the Hells Angels in the 1960s.
Motorcycle club in Vancouver that merged with the Hells Angels in 1983.
Montreal-based motorcycle club; high-ranking member Gregory Woolley was a protégé of Hells Angel Maurice Boucher.
Motorcycle club that patched over to form the Hells Angels' Montreal charter in 1977.
Outlaw motorcycle club that fought the Hells Angels in the Quebec Biker War (1994-2002).
Official support club of the Hells Angels, with chapters in nearly 20 countries.
Support group in Denmark consisting of young males who do not own motorcycles, formed by the Hells Angels.
Support group in Sweden consisting of young males who do not own motorcycles, formed by the Hells Angels.
One of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs in the U.S., alongside Hells Angels, Outlaws, and Bandidos.
One of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs in the U.S., rival of Hells Angels.
One of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs in the U.S., rival of Hells Angels.
Australian outlaw motorcycle club, part of the 'big six' with Hells Angels.
Australian outlaw motorcycle club, part of the 'big six' with Hells Angels.
Outlaw motorcycle club, part of the 'big six' in Australia, rival of Hells Angels.
Australian outlaw motorcycle club, part of the 'big six' with Hells Angels.

edges.context: Otto Friedli broke from Pissed Off Bastards to start Hells Angels.
Alternative theory states Hells Angels founded by Dick White, a Redlands Road Runners member.
San Francisco Hells Angels charter founded by former Market Street Commandos members.
Sacramento Hells Angels charter formed by former Hell Bent for Glory members.
The Hells Angels and East Bay Dragons have a mutual friendship and hang out and ride together.
The Chosen Few said they see no racial animosity and party with the Hells Angels; Hells Angels offered them patches in the 1970s.
Satan's Angels MC merged with the Hells Angels in 1983.
High-ranking Rockers MC member Gregory Woolley was a protégé and bodyguard of Hells Angel Maurice Boucher.
Popeye Moto Club patched over to form the Hells Angels' Montreal charter in 1977.
The Hells Angels fought the Rock Machine in the Quebec Biker War (1994-2002) over drug turf.
The Red Devils are the official support club of the Hells Angels, carrying out crimes on their behalf.
AK81 is a support group in Denmark formed by the Hells Angels, consisting of young males without motorcycles.
The Red & White Crew is a support group in Sweden formed by the Hells Angels.
The Pagans are one of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs and considered rivals of the Hells Angels.
The Outlaws are one of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs and rivals of the Hells Angels.
The Bandidos are one of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs and rivals of the Hells Angels.
The Comanchero are part of the 'big six' in Australia and considered rivals of the Hells Angels.
The Finks are part of the 'big six' in Australia and considered rivals of the Hells Angels.
The Mongols are part of the 'big six' in Australia and considered rivals of the Hells Angels.
The Rebels are part of the 'big six' in Australia and considered rivals of the Hells Angels.
